# Scorched-Earth Erdős Sweep — Turn 125

This is turn 125. Turns 1 through 124 are consolidated in `turns_history__frontiermath_q2_galois.md` (attached). Re-read it before answering.
Also re-read `mega_prompt.md` and `problem_statement.md`.

CRITICAL — use ONLY the files attached to THIS message. Do NOT open, import, retrieve, or cite any file from your File Library / previously uploaded files, even one named similarly (e.g. another `turns_history…md`). Other unrelated problems use similarly named history files in the same account, and importing one will silently switch you onto the wrong problem. If the attached `turns_history__frontiermath_q2_galois.md` appears missing, do NOT continue from partial context. Reply only with `MISSING_REQUIRED_ATTACHMENT` and name the missing file(s); never substitute a library file.

If this turn drifts into hammering one specific case or sub-instance, step back and ask whether the case is revealing a structural pattern that would close many cases at once. Prefer arguments whose conclusion is a uniform statement.

End this response with ESTABLISHED / GAP / NEXT + tag.
Continue with Turn 125.

## Turn 125 — the triangular recurrence has a denominator-free target form

The candidate remains unchanged:

```text
sigma,tau,x0,x1

sigma2 = sigma^40491355905
u0 = (x0*tau)^40491355905
u1 = (x1*tau)^40491355905
d0 = u0*x0^-1
z0 = x0^sigma2
c0 = d0^-1*z0^-1*d0*z0

x0^2*x1^4*u1^-1*(x1^sigma)*c0
```

The tame relation is implicit, and the proof uses the genuine profinite idempotent \(\omega _2\); \(40491355905\) is its prescribed verifier residue. 

Turn 124’s four rank-halving recurrences can be sharpened once more. All deformation-theoretic multiplicities can be eliminated explicitly, leaving integer formulas involving only finite target data and, in the terminal branch, the single local determinant sign. 

Retain
\[
R=\Phi(K),\qquad M=K/R,\qquad
T_0=\operatorname{rad}_{\mathbf F_2[C]}M,\qquad
V=M/T_0,
\]
and
\[
B=Y/R,\qquad C=Y/K,\qquad L=L_Y.
\]

Write \(e_\Gamma(Z)\) for the boundary-framed exact-image count, with every subgroup below carrying the inherited marking and boundary.

### 1. Elimination of the cohomological constants

Proposition 3 gives, for every elementary module \(A\),
\[
|Z^1_\rho(A)|
   =2^{2\dim A+\dim(A^\vee)^C}
   =|A|^2\, |(A^\vee)^C|.
\]

Consequently,
\[
z_M=|M|^2
\]
because \((M^\vee)^C=0\), while
\[
z_R=|R|^2d_R,
\qquad
d_R:=|(R^\vee)^B|,
\]
and
\[
z_{T_0}=|T_0|^2d_0,
\qquad
d_0:=|(T_0^\vee)^C|.
\]

In the terminal determinant branch, \(V\) is nontrivial simple, so \(V^C=0\) and
\[
b=|B^1(V)|=|V|.
\]

Put
\[
\dim_{\mathbf F_2}V=2m,\qquad q=2^m=\sqrt{|V|}.
\]
The determinant zero-count theorem gives
\[
G(Q)=\sum_{\xi\in H^1(V)}(-1)^{Q(\xi)}
      =\varepsilon q,
\]
where
\[
\varepsilon=
\begin{cases}
-1,&V\ \text{unramified},\\
+1,&V\ \text{ramified}.
\end{cases}
\]

Thus the entire analytic input to the recurrence is the one sign
\(\varepsilon\).

### 2. The four denominator-free recurrences

#### Elementary branch: \(R=1\)

Turn 124’s equation becomes
\[
\boxed{
e_\Gamma(Y)
=
|M|^2e_\Gamma(C)
-
\sum_{\substack{J<Y\\J\twoheadrightarrow C}}
e_\Gamma(J).
}
\tag{125.1}
\]

Every proper \(J\) in the sum has marked-kernel order at most \(|L|/2\).

#### Higher-rank Frattini branch: \(|R|\ge4\)

For \(0\ne\lambda\in D_R=(R^\vee)^B\), retain
\[
S_{\Gamma,\lambda}(B)
=
\sum_{\substack{J\le Y_\lambda\\p_\lambda(J)=B}}
e_\Gamma(J).
\]

Substituting \(z_R=|R|^2d_R\) into (124.2) cancels \(d_R\) completely:
\[
\boxed{
e_\Gamma(Y)
=
|R|^2(2-d_R)e_\Gamma(B)
+
\frac{|R|^2}{4}
\sum_{0\ne\lambda\in D_R}
S_{\Gamma,\lambda}(B).
}
\tag{125.2}
\]

The coefficient \(|R|^2/4\) is an integer because \(|R|\ge4\). Every target in every \(S_{\Gamma,\lambda}\) has marked-kernel order at most \(|L|/2\).

#### Rank-one nonzero-edge branch

If
\[
R\simeq C_2,\qquad E(\lambda)\ne0,
\]
then
\[
\boxed{
e_\Gamma(Y)=4e_\Gamma(B)=|R|^2e_\Gamma(B).
}
\tag{125.3}
\]

Here \(B\) has marked-kernel order exactly \(|L|/2\).

#### Terminal rank-one zero-edge branch

Suppose
\[
R\simeq C_2,\qquad E(\lambda)=0.
\]

For \(a\in D_0=(T_0^\vee)^C\), retain
\[
S_{\Gamma,a}(C)
=
\sum_{\substack{H\le C^{\Delta_a}\\p_a(H)=C}}
e_\Gamma(H),
\]
and retain the proper-image correction \(P_\Gamma(B)\) from Turn 124.

Since
\[
|M|=|T_0||V|=|T_0|q^2,
\]
substitution into (124.4) gives
\[
\boxed{
e_\Gamma(Y)
=
|T_0|^2q^3
\left[
\varepsilon\sum_{a\in D_0}S_{\Gamma,a}(C)
+
4(q-\varepsilon d_0)e_\Gamma(C)
\right]
-
P_\Gamma(B).
}
\tag{125.4}
\]

There are now no denominators and no hidden lift multiplicities. Every exact-image count on the right has marked-kernel order at most \(|L|/2\).

### Consequence

Equations (125.1)–(125.4) show that the active-block induction is not merely well-founded; it is an integer triangular algorithm.

All quantities in the recurrence are finite target invariants:

\[
|M|,\ |R|,\ |T_0|,\ d_R,\ d_0,\ q,
\]
the subgroup-incidence sums \(S\) and \(P\), and the single determinant sign
\[
\varepsilon=
\begin{cases}
-1&\text{unramified},\\
+1&\text{ramified}.
\end{cases}
\]

Thus the candidate/local comparison needs no equality of intermediate cohomology objects once these formulas are reached. The two sources satisfy literally the same recurrence with the same terminal scalar-pullback values. The old \(z_M,z_R,z_{T_0},b,G(Q)\) notation can be removed from the final active-block proposition.

**ESTABLISHED**: Derived the denominator-free target-theoretic recurrences (125.1)–(125.4). All deformation multiplicities cancel explicitly; the only surviving non-combinatorial datum is the determinant sign \(\varepsilon=-1\) in the unramified case and \(+1\) in the ramified case. Integrality and rank-halving are now manifest in the displayed formulas.

**GAP**: The private 5402-group verifier remains unrun, and the full proof has not received independent external review.

**NEXT**: Replace Turn 124’s recurrence by (125.1)–(125.4), remove the obsolete \(z\)- and \(b\)-parameters from the manuscript, and freeze the unchanged submission block together with this integer triangular active-block proposition.

[BREAKTHROUGH]
